entries.title: ON THE SPIRIT AND THE LETTER 29.50 on leviticus 18:5
entries.excerpt: The righteousness of the law, of which it is said that if a man does it he shall live in it, is set forth to this end: that every man may recognize his own infirmity. It is not in his own strength or through the letter of the law (which cannot be) but only by receiving through faith the favor of the Justifier that he may attain and do and live in righteousness.

entries.title: HOMILY 76 on leviticus 18:5
entries.excerpt: "The man who carries out the law will find life through it." Scripture did not say he will find life through it, in the sense that through the law he will live in heaven, but he will find life through it to the extent that what he merits, he reaps in the present world.

entries.title: CONFERENCE 21.5.2 on leviticus 18:5
entries.excerpt: For the law promises those who practice it not the rewards of the heavenly kingdom but the consolations of this life when it says, "The one who does these things shall live in them." But the Lord says to his disciples, "Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven."
